title: Sub-Minute Micro-Transaction Probing Before Large Authorization Burst
narrative: Fraudster tests stolen card with 12-15 micro-transactions ($0.50-$2.99) within 90 seconds to verify card validity and available credit, followed immediately by 8-10 larger authorizations ($89-$299) in the subsequent 3 minutes. Initial probes target low-risk MCCs (5499 misc food, 5912 drug stores) with ProductCD=W to avoid merchant friction, then pivot to electronics/digital goods (MCC 5732, 5815) for cash-out phase. All transactions share identical card1, addr1, and P_emaildomain, but dist1 shows impossible geography (2800+ km between first and fifth transaction). Card1_txn_count_1min exceeds 10 before first large authorization hits. Card1_amt_zscore spikes from -0.8 (micro-txns) to +3.2 (large burst) within 4-minute window. Timestamps cluster: 11 transactions between seconds :05-:47 of same minute, followed by 9 more before minute :04 elapsed.
indicators: 12+ transactions with TransactionAmt < $3.00 completed in first 90 seconds
card1_amt_zscore shifts from negative (<-0.5) to strongly positive (>3.0) within 240 seconds
dist1 values indicate 2800+ km geographic separation between transactions occurring within 5-minute window
MCC transition from low-fraud categories (5499, 5912) to high-risk (5732, 5815) mid-burst
10+ transactions share identical card1, addr1, P_emaildomain with no variation in billing details
Total burst duration under 300 seconds with no idle gaps exceeding 20 seconds between authorizations
summary: A card shows 12-15 tiny purchases under $3 in the first 90 seconds, then immediately switches to 8-10 large transactions ($89-$299) in the next 3 minutes, with impossible geographic distances (2800+ km) between charges and merchant categories shifting from groceries to electronics.
reasoning: The fraudster is testing if a stolen card works and how much credit is available using small amounts that won't trigger alerts, then racing to drain the account with big-ticket items before the cardholder or bank notices and blocks the card.
indicator_explanations: Card is being tested with throwaway micro-purchases before the real theft
Rapid-fire transactions pile up before any meaningful dollar amount appears
Spending pattern flips from tiny amounts to suspiciously large charges instantly
Card appears in physically impossible locations within minutes of each other
Purchase types shift from innocent groceries to electronics during the attack
Same card and billing details hammer through identical transaction after transaction
Attack runs continuously without normal pauses a real shopper would take
